programmes.show_description: Respectable dad Stephen wants to see the back of a crude tattoo he had done 20 years ago. Tattoo-mad ex-solider Rob's phallic self-portrait is a step too far.
David Baddiel steers a steamroller towards a representation of his childhood sweetheart, in the hope of impressing Taskmaster Greg Davies.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
In south London, there are feeding faux pas, raunchy revelations and kitchen catastrophes, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
In the West Country, Charlotte and Miguel's Venezuelan menu competes with Pansy and Rob's sophisticated fare and Elle and Dave's organic, wheat-free feast.
The One in the Spanish Sea: Carl aims to marry Rebecca on the Med but she has expectations for a high-class affair. Will Carl's plans make a splash and make Rebecca swoon with delight?
The One with All the Pigs: Billy believes the sty's the limit as he plans a pig-themed wedding involving a world-record parade of porkers. Will bride Steph kick up a stink at Billy's plans?
The Aussie Soap One: Australia-born Adam is taking a risk planning an '80s Aussie soap' wedding when his bride-to-be Rachel is hoping for something classy and sophisticated...
The One Under Edinburgh: Jack plans a haunting, horror-themed wedding, while his bride-to-be Becka dreams of a traditional Scottish affair. Will Jack's concept be a screaming success?
The Big Ibizan Zoo One: Sonny is a party fanatic. How will his bride Jazmin greet his plans for a zoo-themed Ibizan beach party wedding with everyone covered in animal-print body paint?
Colie Edison created every element of the bowling giant Bowlero. Now she goes undercover to find out how the brand is working for staff and customers.
Mayor Adrian Perkins of Shreveport, Louisiana, is the city's second youngest mayor and in charge of 3000 employees. He goes undercover, hoping to change the city's fortunes.
Zayna Flaming Grill (Part 2/2): Gordon returns to California for the conclusion of his challenge to save Zayna Flaming Grill. Can he inject flavour into the tired menu?
Bella Luna: Gordon visits Italian family restaurant Bella Luna, which is struggling with an inexperienced chef and an owner who won't acknowledge the eatery's flaws.
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he's helped: La Galleria 33 in Boston; The Olde Hitching Post in Massachusetts; and the Prohibition Grille in Washington.
Roosevelt Inn: Despite being over a million dollars in debt, the owner of the Roosevelt Inn in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho is busy cracking jokes and playing dress up. Can Gordon help?
Cambridge Hotel: Gordon visits Cambridge, NY, where ex-military man John Imhof runs the Cambridge Hotel like a dictatorship, alienating his staff and guests.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Kathy Burke and Roisin Conaty take on Johnny Vegas and Bob Mortimer. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Vic Reeves take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley does the numbers.
An extended edition of the show with Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock, Jon Richardson and guests Russell Kane, Mark Watson, Louise Redknapp and Example tackling polls and opinions.
Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy statistics quiz, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and guests Johnny Vegas, Jennifer Metcalfe, Joe Lycett and Joey Essex.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Kathy Burke and Roisin Conaty take on Johnny Vegas and Bob Mortimer. John Cooper Clarke joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Vic Reeves take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. David O'Doherty joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley does the numbers.
Cooking the Books: Bookshop based comedy. When his dodgy accountant goes on the run, Bernard has to fill in his own taxes, which isn't easy when you're mathematically illiterate.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
The One in the Sky: The sky's the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai who plans to have a wedding in the sky, even though his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.
The Sk8er Boi One: Will Andy's plans for a skater-themed wedding make his bride Bobby's wheels spin? Or will there be broken bones and broken hearts on the big day?
The Obstacle Course One: Chris wants his bride to jump through hoops, and plans to turn his wedding into an obstacle course. But Charlotte dreams of a traditional family wedding.
Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modell's Sporting Goods, America's oldest family-owned company selling athletics apparel and equipment, goes incognito in his own firm.
Kat Cole, the president of Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from over 900 bakeries in 40 countries, goes incognito among her own workforce.
In Devon, there's a 'Britalian' mash-up, stomach-churning confessions and generous helpings of criticism, as three rival couples put their hosting skills to the ultimate test.
Three couples compete in and around Newcastle, in a week featuring refined dining, an energetic evening, bickering and an eclectic menu.
Three couples compete to host the best dinner party in and around Newcastle. There's Scottish fare and assumptions about accents, and a dessert nearly goes up in flames!
In Hull, image-conscious Jay and Heather go all-out American. Thrifty Mike and Stef serve a school dinner throwback. And is Steve and Stephanie's fine dining fare a little too fancy?
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon visits the historic Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont, which looks like a million dollars but smells of sewage...
Juniper Hill Inn (Part 2/2): The issues keep coming at the Juniper Hill Inn in Vermont. Can Gordon help the staff and owners to communicate? Or will he be forced to abandon ship?
River Rock Inn: Gordon checks into the River Rock Inn in Pennsylvania, where owner Ken Pisciotta, who's drowning in debt, can't convince guests to stay in his neglected hotel.
The Keating: Gordon checks into The Keating, a boutique hotel in San Diego, California. Its owner has blown millions on design but hasn't spent on items the guests really need.
Meson de Mesilla: Can Gordon Ramsay fix Meson de Mesilla, a Tuscan-style hotel in Las Cruces, New Mexico, that's falling flat?
Joe Wilkinson, Alex Brooker, Kevin Bridges, Judi Love and Brian Butterfield join Jimmy, Susie and Rachel for another helping of chaos, comedy and Countdown.
In Limboland, somewhere between expectation and reality, Bill ponders the true meaning of happiness with his trademark wit, musicality - and a remarkably downbeat version of Happy Birthday!
Cooking the Books: Bookshop based comedy. When his dodgy accountant goes on the run, Bernard has to fill in his own taxes, which isn't easy when you're mathematically illiterate.
Manny's First Day: A drunk Bernard offers Manny a job at the bookshop then tries to get rid of him by putting him on a one-day trial.
